Guideline: Use clap derive for argument parsing
Description: Define CLI arguments as a struct with clap derive macros for type-safe, documented argument parsing
Do: Use #[derive(Parser)] with doc comments as help text; use subcommands for complex CLIs; use #[arg] for fine-tuning
Dont: Manually parse std::env::args; use string matching for flags

Guideline: Use colored and tabled for rich output
Description: Provide colored and structured terminal output for better user experience
Do: Use colored or console for colors; tabled for tables; indicatif for progress bars; dialoguer for prompts
Dont: Print plain unformatted text; use println! for everything

Description: Use anyhow::Result for CLI error handling with proper exit codes and user-friendly messages
Do: Use anyhow::Context for error chains; set exit code based on error type; print errors to stderr
Dont: Use .unwrap() in CLI apps; print errors to stdout; crash with raw panic messages

Description: Provide interactive prompts, selections, and confirmations for user-driven CLI workflows
Do: Use Confirm for yes/no; Select for choice lists; Input for text; Password for secrets
Dont: Force all input through CLI arguments; implement custom stdin reading

Guideline: Support config files with env overrides
Description: Load configuration from files with environment variable overrides following 12-factor principles
Do: Use config crate for hierarchical config; support TOML/YAML + env vars; merge with CLI args
Dont: Hardcode configurations; require a config file with no env fallback

Description: Use assert_cmd for integration testing of CLI binaries with stdout/stderr assertions
Do: Test CLI as a subprocess; verify exit codes, stdout, stderr; use predicates for flexible matching
Dont: Only test library functions; skip testing the actual binary interaction
